publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-11-06 00:16:21 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Biographical files, correspondence, research subject files and notes, scientific papers, presentations, lectures, handwritten lecture notebooks and other course-related material, publications, reviews, drafts, reprints, administrative files, photographs, slides, DVDs, CDs, transparencies, and flash drives of the Goldwin Smith Professor of Physics Emeritus at Cornell University, Vinay Ambegaokar (1934- ), document his research, teaching, and administrative activities, dating from 1942-2018.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Vinay Ambegaokar papers, #14-22-4461. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Vinay Ambegaokar Papers document the extensive research, teaching, and administrative activities and publications of the Goldwin Smith Professor of Physics Emeritus at Cornell University, Vinay Ambegaokar (1934- ), dating from 1942-2018. The collection includes biographical files, such as professor's personal writing and poems, extensive correspondence related to research, publications, proposal evaluations, and more; research material with subject notes, scientific papers, preparation material for publications lectures, and presentations; handwritten lecture notebooks and other teaching material, including syllabus, assignments, lectures, final examinations, problem sets, and notes; publications by the professor and by other researchers; administrative files, including the Andrew D. White Professor-at-Large (PAL) Program. Finally, there are a few photographs, slides, a DVD about the power of the sun, CDs, transparencies, and flash drives with video lectures, photographs, and several documents, most of which already exist in print form throughout the collection.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Vinay Ambegaokar (1934, January 16- ) was born in Nagpur, Madhya Pradesh, India. In 1956, he earned his B.S., S.M. Mechanical Engineering Honors Program from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT). Four years later (1960), he completed his doctorate in Theoretical Physics from the Carnegie Institute of Technology, focusing on the Electromagnetic properties of insulators from a many-particle point of view. During his post-doctoral studies (1960 to 1962), he served as a Ford Foundation Research Associate at the Bohr Institute in Copenhagen, Denmark. Professor Vinay Ambegaokar taught in Cornell's School of Physics from 1962 until his retirement in July 2007 and supervised 20 Ph.D. theses. From 1969 to 1971, he also served as the Director of the Research Institute for Theoretical Physics, University of Helsinki, Finland. His research focused on condensed matter and low-temperature physics, some aspects of disordered metallic conductors, quantum information and its loss through decoherence, and mathematical ways of describing these phenomena. Professor Vinay Ambegaokar collaborated with several companies and institutions in the US, Europe, India, and more, such as the North American-Rockwell Science Center; Brookhaven National Laboratory; IBM Watson Research Center; Institute of Theoretical Physics, UCSB; Collège de France; University of Karlsruhe; NORDITA, Copenhagen; University of Florida; All Souls College, Oxford; Bohr Institute, Copenhagen; Raman Research Institute, Bangalore, India and more. He was a fellow of professional, scientific, and honorary societies, such as the American Physical Society (1979), the Alfred P. Sloan Foundation (1965-1967), and the J. S. Guggenheim (1983-84). He is a recipient of the Medal of the University of Helsinki in 1971, the Medal of the Collège de France in 1986, the Humboldt Foundation Senior U. S. Scientist (1986, 1990), and the John Bardeen Prize in 2015. He got married to Saga Mirjam Vuori on June 26, 1956. He has two daughters, Maia Anneli and Liisa Ambegaokar Grigorov.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: 1) Gould, Christopher M. Superfluid He3 in Narrow Cylinders, 1979-01 Ph.D. Thesis. Report: 4024. Research Group Prof. D.M. Lee, LASSP, Cornell University. 2) Woo, James Wing Fai. Transport Equations for Superconductors and Thermal Conductivity of Superconductors, 1966-06 Ph.D. Thesis. Report: 499. Advisor: V. Ambegaokar. Material Science Center, Cornell University. 3) Janko, Boldizsar. Number Parity Effects in Superconductors, 1996 Ph.D. Thesis. Advisor: V. Ambegaokar. Cornell University. 4) Von Delft, Jan. 2 Channel Kondo Scaling in Metal Nanoconstrictions. A conformal Field Theory Calculation of Scaling Function, 1995 Ph.D. Thesis. Advisor: V. Ambegaokar. Cornell University. 5) Ambegaokar, V. Reasoning about Luck: Probability and its Uses in Physics. Cambridge: Cambridge University Press, 2017

controlaccess.subject: Physics.

Correspondence with colleagues and a wine order

scopecontent.p: Handwritten letters to his wife, Saga.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sherwood College certifications; Trinity College of Music, London; John Bardeen Prize 2015; Bachelor of Science in Mechanical Engineering (1956); Ph.D. in Physics, Carnegie Institute of Technology (1960); Gordon Research Conference (1977) and more.

scopecontent.p: Includes a speech about Walter Kohn as a Ph.D. advisor, Holcomb's Theology, and more.

did.unittitle: Silver plate from the Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur, Prof. Jagadishwar Mahanty. Distinguished Lecture Series in Physics
Voo Doo magazine, Art and Science newsletter, and other publications

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 38, No 1. February 1955. Dr. V. Ambegaokar was the Editor of this volume.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A report on all the research activities during the almost six months period, Prof. Vinay Ambegaokar stayed in Oxford, UK is included. In addition, there is a Royalty statement for the year 1997 sent by the Cambridge University Press (referring to his book Reasoning about luck). Comments on his book by George Rutter, Eton College. The lecture "Reflections, Reminiscences and Ramblings on the Theme: Memory and Creativity".

did.unittitle: All Souls College, Oxford University, living

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten letters, a poem by C.C. Cummings and Encaenia 1998, Special Guest Night table organization, and dinner menus.

scopecontent.p: Correspondence on proposal evaluations, travels, publications, administrative issues, conferences, committees, research projects, and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence on courses, seminars, memorandums, and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence on publication reviews, letters of recommendation, and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Professor Vinay Ambegaokar's resume is included. Correspondence on publication reviews, requests for letters of recommendation, and more. There is also a nomination form for the award of the 1983 Nobel prize for Physics from the Royal Academy of Sciences, Nobel Committee for Physics. Vinay Ambegaokar was a nominator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence on publication reviews, symposiums, professor's sabbatical and other professional travels, research projects and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence on publications, letters of recommendation, award nominations, proposals and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Professor's biographical note and a text titled "Growing up in a vanished India." Correspondence on the Bethe Lecture Series, publication reviews, conferences, and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence related to the Graduate School, the University Faculty office, and other Departments and Offices within Cornell Campus. Material related to the Nobel Prize in Physics (2006) and more.

did.unittitle: Publications, awards, and communication with other institutions
Research Institute for Theoretical Physics, University of Helsinki
Research Institute for Theoretical Physics, University of Helsinki

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between professor Vinay Ambegaokar when he was a professor at the Research Institute for Theoretical Physics, University of Helsinki, Finland, with several people or organizations, like Cornell University, New York University, University of Minnesota, University of Washington, The Israel Science Service inc., the Italian Embassy, and several other institutes. Several topics are coved. Inquiries for letters of recommendation, invitations to conferences, lectures, several research issues, publications, and more. A few documents are in the French language.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Seminar on Case Western Reserve University, Cleveland, Ohio. 1967, June 19-23. Includes 14 photographs of the seminar's participants, a list of the participants, transparencies, printed slides, a report, handwritten notes, a notebook and relevant correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A list of seminars is in the original folder.

scopecontent.p: Includes Dr. V. Ambekaogar's reply to an article about a new theoretical approach for calculating various properties of disordered interacting metallic systems, published by Dr. Golubev and Zaikin. Also, there is a final reply by the Physical Review Letters on the manuscript "Universal gap..proximity effect".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mattias Troyer, Theoretische Physik, ETH Zurich, Switzerland. Main article titled "Estimating errors in Monte Carlo simulations of the Ehrenfest model".

scopecontent.p: Includes an inventory of all his publications.

scopecontent.p: American Institute of Physics Translation Program. Included professor's translated articles in Russian. Relevant material in Box 2 Folder 18, Box 4 Folder 1, Box 4 Folder 14, Box 10 Folder 26.

scopecontent.p: Chairman, University Music Committee. Cornell University.

did.unittitle: VI. A. Andrew D. White Professor-at-Large (PAL) Program

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included a list of all the Andrew D. White Professors-at-Large as of July 1, 1978.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: People who named Andrew D. White Professor-at-Large in 1981 are Michelangelo Antonioni (Film Director), Adrienne Rich (Poet), Laszlo Lovasz (Mathematician), Paul Greengard (Department of Pharmacology, Yale University). Correspondence to Frank H.T. Rhodes, Cornell University President.

did.unittitle: PAL, correspondence, and memoranda on annual reports and finances

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collège de France, Physique de la matière condensée, Paris, France.

did.unittitle: PAL, selection committee minutes, memorandum and correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: List of Andrew D. White Professors-at-Large for the period 1965-1983.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Jacques Derrida, Romila Thapar, Richard Garwin and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal photographs with the professor and his wife at the Aurora Inn in Aurora, NY, from 2011. Personal Writing, related to Series I. Biographical Notes. Keynote Presentations related to Series III. Research Material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Presentations, talks (some video recorded), scientific papers, Landau-related documents (LASSP, see relevant material in Box 2, Folder 18, Box 4 Folder 1, Box 4 Folder 14, Box 10 Folder 26); tickets to Naples, Italy; correspondence with Troyer on a paper; retirement symposium, several keynotes on papers; scientific presentations in tiffs. Ford- O' Connell double-slit probabilities. PDF-s, ppt-s, tiffs. Covered topics include Quantum Brownian Motion, Dissipative Oscillator, Entropy, and Time. Related to Series III. Research Material.

did.unittitle: Hanbury impressions, Ludwig Tewordt, Dietrich Einzel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: PowerPoint. Related to Series IV. Teaching material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Video Lecture. Related to Series IV. Teaching material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Related to Series I. Biographical Files.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A documentary on solar electricity created by Nobel Laureates Walter Kohn and Alan Heeger. Related to Series III. Research Material
